claims.claim: As of mid-2026, LLMs that handle Hebrew well include Anthropic Claude (Sonnet/Opus), the OpenAI GPT-5.x family, and Google Gemini 3.x. GPT-4o/4.1 and Gemini 2.x were retired in early 2026. Dicta-LM 3.0 is the current Hebrew-native option.
claims.raw_snippet: GPT-4o and GPT-4.1 were retired from ChatGPT in early 2026; the current lineup is the GPT-5.x family.

claims.claim: The WhatsApp Cloud API uses Graph API v25.0; messages are sent via POST https://graph.facebook.com/v25.0/{PHONE_NUMBER_ID}/messages.
claims.raw_snippet: Graph API v25.0 is the current version. WhatsApp Cloud API endpoints are versioned under graph.facebook.com.

claims.claim: WhatsApp Cloud API platform documentation (setup, message types, webhooks) lives on Meta's business-messaging docs.
claims.raw_snippet: The WhatsApp Business Platform Cloud API lets businesses send and receive messages; docs cover API version, message types, and webhook format.

claims.claim: WhatsApp moved to per-message pricing on 1 July 2025; per-message rates by country and category change regularly, so do not hardcode them.
claims.raw_snippet: WhatsApp Business pricing is per message as of July 1, 2025; rates vary by country and category.

claims.claim: The Telegram Bot API is documented at core.telegram.org/bots/api; python-telegram-bot (>=21, async) wraps it.
claims.raw_snippet: The Bot API is an HTTP-based interface for building bots for Telegram.

claims.claim: python-telegram-bot v20+/21+ uses an async API; it is the standard Python Telegram bot library.
claims.raw_snippet: python-telegram-bot provides a pure Python, asynchronous interface for the Telegram Bot API.

claims.claim: YAP (Yet Another Parser, OnlpLab) is a Hebrew morphological analyzer/parser usable for Hebrew NLP.
claims.raw_snippet: YAP is a morphological analyzer and dependency parser for Hebrew from the Open University NLP Lab.

claims.claim: Telegram bots are created and configured via BotFather commands: /newbot (create), /setdescription, /setprivacy.
claims.raw_snippet: Use BotFather's /newbot to create a bot, /setdescription to set its description, and /setprivacy to control whether the bot sees all group messages or only commands.

claims.claim: your-domain.com/webhook is an illustrative placeholder for the operator's own webhook callback URL.
claims.raw_snippet: Replace your-domain.com/webhook with your own HTTPS webhook endpoint registered with the WhatsApp/Telegram platform.

claims.claim: tracking.example.com with a {{1}} placeholder illustrates a WhatsApp template button URL with a dynamic parameter; example.com is a reserved documentation domain.
claims.raw_snippet: WhatsApp template buttons can carry a dynamic URL parameter {{1}}; tracking.example.com is an illustrative placeholder host.

claims.claim: WhatsApp Business accounts are managed in Meta Business Manager.
claims.raw_snippet: Meta Business Manager (business.facebook.com) is where WhatsApp Business accounts, phone numbers, and message templates are managed.

claims.claim: The WhatsApp send-message endpoint with a shell/env variable for the phone number id.
claims.raw_snippet: POST https://graph.facebook.com/v25.0/{PHONE_NUMBER_ID}/messages sends a WhatsApp message; PHONE_NUMBER_ID is substituted at runtime.
